chats_view.title: Chat
chats_view.subtitle: Talk to Jarvis or type — both go through the same event bus.
chats_view.empty_subtitle: Say "Hey Jarvis" or type your message below. Jarvis dispatches sub-agents, calls tools, and gives you a voice back.
chats_view.speak_started: Listening — continue this conversation by voice.
chats_view.speak_unavailable: Voice isn't available in this mode.
chats_view.select_or_new: Pick a conversation on the left, or start a new one.

tasks_view.title: Tasks
tasks_view.subtitle: Persistent task queue — reminders, harness dispatches, tool calls.
tasks_view.empty_hint: Currently tasks can only be created via REST API — POST /api/tasks with a TaskSpec (trigger + action). Voice commands like "remind me in 2 hours" and an inline form will follow.

skills_view.title: Skills
skills_view.subtitle: Built-in and user skills with voice and event triggers.

review_view.title: Review
review_view.subtitle: Curator pending reviews — accept or reject memory candidates.
review_view.empty_subtitle: When the curator extracts new memory candidates, they appear here.

sessions_view.title: Transcription
sessions_view.subtitle: Recent voice sessions, turn by turn.
sessions_view.empty_subtitle: After a voice conversation with Jarvis, the transcripts appear here.

terminal_view.title: Terminal
terminal_view.subtitle: Persistent shell session — stays alive while you switch sections.
terminal_view.select_shell_hint: Select a shell and press Start.
terminal_view.status_idle: Ready · {0} · click Start to spawn the shell

docs_view.title: Docs
docs_view.subtitle: Project documentation, architecture decisions, and bug log.

mcps_view.title: MCPs
mcps_view.subtitle: Model Context Protocol servers — external tools Jarvis can call.
mcps_view.intro: Jarvis manages MCP servers automatically. Activate one with the switch — the connection is checked first. For manual edits open mcp.json.
mcps_view.empty_description: MCP servers are configured exclusively via mcp.json (Claude Desktop format). Edit it directly, or import the full Claude Desktop config.
mcps_view.empty_tip: Tip: Tell Jarvis later "install MCP X" — it will add it to mcp.json itself.
mcps_view.config_format_hint: Claude Desktop format. Per server: command, args, optional env and enabled. Env values with $-prefix are resolved from the Credential Manager (e.g. "$GMAIL_TOKEN").

board_view.title: Board
board_view.subtitle: Your mastery dashboard. Local, no cloud, no streak pressure.
board_view.activity_description: Last 365 days. Voice, chat, tools, tasks and app starts. Each cell = one day.
board_view.ai_profile_description: What Jarvis has learned about you. Updated evolutionarily on Sundays.
board_view.ai_profile_first_bio_hint: First short bio appears after the first active day, deeper observations on Sundays.
board_view.feedback_noted: Noted. Will affect the next bio.
board_view.tools_chart_empty: No tool usage in this window
board_view.backend_local_only: Federation is disabled (local-only mode). Enable in jarvis.toml: [board.federation] enabled = true.
board_view.no_records_yet: No records yet — they appear automatically once data exists.

profile_view.title: Profile
profile_view.subtitle: Your USER.md, people you know, and pending memory reviews.
profile_view.no_name_yet: Jarvis doesn't know your name yet. Tell him "My name is …".
profile_view.reviews_disabled_body: This session's fact curator writes straight to the wiki vault and doesn't use a review queue. Candidates show up here once a provider with the classic memory integration is running.
profile_view.hero_tagline: What Jarvis has learned about you — from your conversations.
profile_view.no_user_hint: Jarvis doesn't know your name yet. Just tell him what to call you — the curator picks up the rest from your conversations over time.
profile_view.people_empty_body: When you tell Jarvis about family, friends or colleagues, the curator automatically creates a file under data/workspace/people/.
profile_view.person_detail_hint: Pick a person on the left to see details.
profile_view.person_file_hint: Observations and full notes live in people/{0}.md.
profile_view.reviews_empty_body: Every extracted fact was high-confidence and applied directly. When the curator is unsure, candidates land here.
profile_view.raw_empty: (File empty — the curator hasn't written anything yet)
profile_view.raw_editing_hint: Editing the source file directly. The YAML frontmatter between the --- drives the clusters above.
profile_view.raw_frontmatter_warning: Saved — but the YAML frontmatter couldn't be parsed. The clusters stay empty until you fix it.
profile_view.avatar_hint: Click the frame to upload a picture of yourself.

communication.label: Communication
communication.description: How Jarvis talks to you: directness, humor, formality.

relationship.label: Relationship
relationship.description: How Jarvis gives feedback, how the dynamic runs.

apikeys_view.title: API Keys & Providers
apikeys_view.subtitle: Keys live in Windows Credential Manager. Codex uses the official ChatGPT/Codex login.
apikeys_view.needs_codex: First connect Codex or save API key, then activate
apikeys_view.needs_key: First save API key, then activate
apikeys_view.security_notice: Keys are stored via keyring in the Windows Credential Manager. Jarvis reads them at runtime via get_secret() — they never end up in jarvis.toml. OpenAI Codex uses only the official Codex CLI for ChatGPT login; Jarvis never asks for ChatGPT credentials.

settings_view.title: Settings
settings_view.subtitle: Wake-word, hotkeys, safety policies, autostart.

rows.ptt_description: Hold to talk, release to send. Works without the wake word.
rows.scope_description: "Jarvis, work in folder X" pins context until released.
rows.autostart_description: Jarvis starts in tray after login.
rows.toasts_description: Show "Jarvis opened X" notification when voice controls the UI.

wake_word.title: Wake Word
wake_word.description: Activation phrase for voice mode. Pick a quick phrase or type your own.
wake_word.degraded_warning: The chosen phrase isn't supported by the selected engine — it will fall back to a different engine.
wake_word.needs_whisper_hint: An arbitrary phrase needs the local-Whisper extra. Without it the engine will degrade to a built-in quick phrase.

engine_options.stt_match: STT match (any phrase, needs local Whisper)

autostart.title: Start at login
autostart.description: Launch Jarvis automatically when you log in, so "Hey Jarvis" works right after a reboot. Turn it off here any time.
autostart.enabled_toast: Autostart enabled — Jarvis will launch at login.
autostart.unsupported: Autostart is not available on this host (e.g. a headless server with no desktop session).

assistant_name.title: Assistant Name
assistant_name.description: How the assistant refers to itself in replies. Leave empty to follow the wake word.
assistant_name.label: Name

hotkey.title: Push-to-Talk Hotkey
hotkey.description: Click Record and press your key combination — hold it to talk, release to send. Or pick a safe suggestion below.
hotkey.recording: Press your keys now… (Esc to cancel)

settings_view.codex_description: Manual path for Windows PATH issues. Empty uses 'codex' from PATH.
settings_view.safety_description: Patterns executed without confirmation, even at risk-tier 'ask'. Avoids confirmation fatigue.

telephony_view.title: Telephony
telephony_view.subtitle: Call Jarvis on a real phone number. Same STT, Brain, and Charon voice as "Hey Jarvis".
telephony_view.not_configured_hint: Set your Twilio Account SID, Auth Token, and phone number below to finish setup, then run "Test connection".
telephony_view.unavailable_body: The optional Twilio integration is not available in this install. Add the telephony extras group, then restart Jarvis.

creds.title: Credentials & config
creds.greeting_placeholder: Leave empty for the Jarvis butler default

scripts.title: Setup scripts
scripts.subtitle: Run these to expose Jarvis publicly and provision the Twilio number. Copy a command with the button.
scripts.empty: No setup scripts reported by the backend yet.

calls.title: Recent calls
calls.empty: No calls yet. Once someone dials your number, the turns appear here.

debug_view.title: Debug
debug_view.subtitle: Live event stream from the orchestrator.

previews_view.title: Previews
previews_view.subtitle: Live preview servers Jarvis spawned for you.

outputs_view.title: Outputs
outputs_view.subtitle: Files Jarvis or sub-agents wrote for you.

common.open_in_editor: Open Markdown file in OS default editor

sessions.select_one: Select a session on the left.
sessions.no_turns: This session has no turns — probably only a wake-word.

provider_switcher.loading_hint: Provider list is loading — if it stays empty, check the backend.

cli_connect.login_aborted: {0}: login check failed after 5 min. Restart manually if needed.

apikeys_codex.needs_codex_full: {0}: first connect Codex or save API key, then activate.
apikeys_codex.needs_key_full: {0}: first save API key, then activate.

terminal_xterm.install_no_binary: {0}: install completed (exit 0) but binary not found. PATH may not be refreshed.
terminal_xterm.install_failed: {0}: install failed (exit {1}). Try a different method.

languages_view.title: Languages
languages_view.subtitle: Choose the UI language and the language Jarvis replies in.
languages_view.ui_hint: Affects every label, button and message in the app.
languages_view.reply_hint: Which language Jarvis speaks when answering you. Proper names, code identifiers and technical terms stay in their original form.

en.label: English
en.description: Use English for every label and button.

de.label: Deutsch (German)
de.description: Use German for every label and button.

es.label: Español (Spanish)
es.description: Use Spanish for every label and button.

reply_options.auto: Jarvis mirrors whichever language you speak or type.

languages_view.recognition_text: Whisper detects whichever language you speak — no manual switch needed.
languages_view.override_text: Say "Jarvis, reply in German" or "Por favor en español" — Jarvis switches for the current session without changing this setting.
